h2h_summary: Senegal leads the all-time head-to-head record with their only meeting resulting in a 2-1 Senegal victory in a 2006 friendly.
team_style_home: Norway play an aggressive 4-3-3 with high pressing and rapid vertical transitions, built around Erling Haaland as a central target with Odegaard pulling strings in the advanced midfield role.
team_style_away: Senegal deploy a physical 4-3-3 with high pressing and counter-attacking pace, relying on Mane's deep-lying creativity behind Jackson and wide players who attack defenders in transition.
wc_history_home: Norway appeared at three World Cups (1938, 1994, 1998), with their best result being the Round of 16 in 1998, and return in 2026 after a 28-year absence having qualified with a perfect eight wins from eight games.
wc_history_away: Senegal made a stunning debut at the 2002 World Cup reaching the quarter-finals after defeating defending champions France, and reached the Round of 16 again in 2022 before losing 3-0 to England.
group_context: Group I is headlined by two-time runners-up France, making the second qualification spot a fierce battle between Senegal and Norway, with the June 22 clash between those two sides widely seen as the decisive game for knockout-stage progression; Iraq are heavy underdogs in their first World Cup in 40 years.
strengths_home: Elite goalscoring threat through Haaland, who scored 16 goals in qualifying
Relentless high-press system that disrupts opposition build-up
Fast vertical transitions from defence to attack with quality in all positions
strengths_away: Experienced and well-organised defensive block anchored by Koulibaly
Multiple attacking threats across the front three with Premier League pedigree
Physical intensity and counter-attacking pace through Mane, Jackson and Sarr
